title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 7th May 2026 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Definitely buy for medium-to-long term; seasonal stock with highest sales this summer, so Q1 will be good.
recommendations.detail: Havells can definitely be bought for the medium to long term. He treats it as one of the seasonal stocks - because of the branding they have across fans and everything else, the highest sales come in this summer itself, so the first quarter will also be good. (Ramakrishna added a technical caveat that it looks weak and may consolidate before performing.)

recommendations.note: Good numbers; stock doing better since results, forming higher tops and higher bottoms.
recommendations.detail: AGI Greenpac can definitely be bought for the medium to long term. The results came in well and, since the results, the stock has been doing better as well. It is forming higher tops and higher bottoms, so he said definitely buy it.

recommendations.note: Not at current price near 52-week high; pick it up on a fall below 700.
recommendations.detail: Not right away - buy Granules when a bit of a decline comes, since it has run to its 52-week highs. It was at a take-off stage around 600 and after a long consolidation broke out there, adding about 150 since. Technically he thinks a fall below 700 should come, and on that correction he would definitely pick it up. The second generation of management is doing extremely well and the shift to a younger management may bring changes; market cap is now close to Rs 20,000 crore. He called it a silent performer that Hyderabad-pharma coverage under-plays versus SMS, Laurus, Natco and Dr Reddy's.

recommendations.note: Stable Murugappa Group stock; no excitement but stable returns - medium-to-long term only.
recommendations.detail: Carborundum is a stable stock from the Murugappa Group. There is no excitement in it, but there will be stable returns, because in the engineering space there are only two or three stocks of this kind. He said definitely buy, but only for medium-to-long-term stable returns.

recommendations.note: Not in favour; work there and take the salary, but invest that money in some other company.
recommendations.detail: Asked by an EPAM software engineer whether to continue investing via a 15% ESPP discount, he said he is not in favour of investing in EPAM. He repeated his workshop principle: whatever industry we work in, we know it better than outsiders, so we should rationalise our investments with that knowledge. The mailer already knows his company is an underperformer, which is why he is asking for a second opinion. Work in EPAM, take the salary there, and invest that money in some other company.

recommendations.note: Book partial profits on the 75-to-110 relief rally; regulatory changes not favourable to wind power.
recommendations.detail: For a holder who bought at Rs 87, he said book partial profits. He does not think the stock will perform greatly in the short term, since recent regulatory changes are not favourable to wind power companies - not negative, but not favourable either. The move from 75 to 110 is a relief rally, so it is better for the holder to exit somewhat here.

recommendations.note: Certainly invest; could become a $1 trillion company - growth story likely to run another 3-5 years.
recommendations.detail: Asked about investing in US memory-chip makers and selecting Micron, he said certainly do it. Micron could in future become a $1 trillion company and we needn't be surprised - it is that fast-growing. The growth story appears likely to continue for another three to five years.

recommendations.price: exit close to buy price; capped below 4,500
recommendations.note: Don't average; exit even at marginal losses - can't cross 4,500 and still a 120-PE stock.
recommendations.detail: He said don't average. Exit even close to the buy price or at marginal losses, because ever since a broking house put out a negative report, the stock has recovered from that but has not been able to cross the 4,500 level. It is also still a high-PE stock at around 120 PE.

recommendations.note: Refused to name one - take two, three or four: KIMS a good choice, plus Fortis and leader Apollo.
recommendations.detail: Asked to name one hospital stock for a Rs 50,000 medium-term investment, he said many stocks are performing splendidly and naming just one is hard - he would rather take two, three or four. KIMS is one good choice, along with Fortis Healthcare and, in a leadership role, Apollo Hospitals. All of these have created great wealth.

recommendations.price: hold until 52-week high; can touch 100 again
recommendations.note: Results not out; interesting developments per his sources - hold to the 52-week high despite promoter's politics.
recommendations.detail: The results have not come yet, but based on his sources some interesting developments are happening in the company. He was candid on governance: the promoter is more busy with politics than managing the company - he is a minister in Telangana, and both father and son have left the company aside. Even so, there is a possibility the stock can touch 100 again, so he said hold until the 52-week high.

recommendations.note: Contradicted 'nothing great' - definitely good results; record full-year revenue; attractive after the correction.
recommendations.detail: Against the anchor's read that the results were nothing great, he said they were definitely good results - from a flat position it clearly showed about 15% growth, and on a full-year basis the company achieved record revenue for the first time. Growth from such a record-revenue base will be very easy. He said hold, adding that after the recent correction the stock is looking very attractive at current levels.
